label: Jun 15 – Jul 14, 2026
period_mismatch_note: This report covers 30 days vs the prior report's 40 days. Raw-count deltas are deflated ~25% by the shorter window; rate metrics (CPL, CTR, conv rate) remain comparable.

summary.narrative: The strongest lead month on record — and on a shorter window than last report. Combined spend of $20.9K produced 99 leads at $211 each, a 21% improvement in unit economics. Meta did the heavy lifting: 80 leads at $127 CPL, with the runaway Sarasota prospecting problem fixed ($473 → $87 CPL) and the new Brochure Download campaign delivering leads at $43. Two reservations landed this period — the first since April. The watch item is Google: search conversions halved to 19 and the Competitors campaign spent $2,979 without a single conversion. Organic demand keeps building (queries +24% on a shorter window), though average search position slipped to 13.9. Phone coverage remains the weak link at a 62% answer rate.

google_ads.ctr_note: Search-only CTR (comparable to prior periods): 1,663 clicks on 24,054 search impressions. Blended CTR incl. Display is 0.6% on 369K impressions.
google_ads.demographics_skew: 55-64 leads clicks, then 65+; gender near-even (F 35 / M 33 / Unknown 32)
google_ads.top_ad: Mira Mar Residences | Sarasota Luxury Condos | Historic Sarasota Icon Reborn

meta_ads.top_creative: Image_Building (Brochure Download) — 15 leads at $39.69 CPL; static imagery keeps outperforming video for lead gen

vs_2026-06-04.period_note: Current period is 30 days; prior was 40 days. Count deltas are deflated ~25% by the shorter window; rates are comparable.

spark_reservations.note: first reservations since April — prior period had 0

recommendations.headline: Pause or rework the Google Competitors campaign
recommendations.detail: $2,979 spent this period with zero conversions (357 clicks at $8.34 each). Redirect that budget to Non-Brand Sarasota — still the top Google converter with 14 leads — or to Meta, where CPL is a quarter of Google's.

recommendations.headline: Scale the Brochure Download + Remarketing combo
recommendations.detail: The new Brochure Download campaign produced 14 leads at $43 each and Remarketing 9 at $33 — the two cheapest lead sources across both platforms. Feeder Market Segmented is 6-8x more expensive ($267 CPL); shift a portion of its $6.1K budget toward the winners.

recommendations.headline: Fix phone coverage before season
recommendations.detail: Answer rate fell again, from 76% to 62% (5 of 13 calls missed). Volume is low enough that every missed call is a potentially wasted $200+ lead. Confirm ring-through and voicemail follow-up ownership.

recommendations.headline: Diagnose the Google conversion falloff
recommendations.detail: Search conversions halved (39 → 19) on similar spend, and conversion rate slipped below 1%. Review landing-page changes, form tracking, and search-term match quality this month — the traffic is still there (CTR held at 6.9%).

recommendations.headline: Recover search position with Sarasota-first content
recommendations.detail: Queries grew 24% (on a shorter window) but average position slipped from 9.5 to 13.9, and much of the new impression volume is mismatched "Miramar, FL apartment" searches with 0 clicks. Publish Sarasota-specific content to rebuild position 8-10.

recommendations.headline: Press the reservation momentum
recommendations.detail: Two reservations landed this period — the first since April — and the warm bench is rich: Fanny Lange ($5-7M budget, call July 20), William Tremko (visit planned, favorites picked), Denise Baskerville (90-day buying window). Prioritize in-person visits for these three in the next two weeks.
